warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis for pyridine was performed using the DFT method with the B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set (tier `standard`) in the gas phase. The calculation was performed using PySCF as the backend. The input geometry was provided from the user-supplied file `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz`. The molecule had a charge of 0 and a multiplicity of 1.

The most electrophilic site (highest Fukui f+) was found to be **N (atom 1)** with an f+ value of **0.48422572**.
The most nucleophilic site (highest Fukui f-) was found to be **C (atom 4)** with an f- value of **0.1182626**.

A plot visualizing the Fukui reactivity is available at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/argo_gemini-2.5-flash/20260706-224159_pyridine_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`.
